laws.title: Public Law 22-162 | Judicial Fairness Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section IV expressly amends 28 U.S.C. 1253, 1257, and 1291. Section V directly amends other Public Laws rather than the Code and is out of scope for a Code-text operation. The act itself is not repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and general provisions.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Judicial Fairness Act."

provisions.text_summary: Amends 28 U.S.C. 1253.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section text with the approved Supreme Court review provision.
provisions.evidence: Section IV(1) of the Act amends 28 U.S.C. 1253.
provisions.notes: Approved plan exists and is sequenced after PL-17-133.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 28 U.S.C. 1257.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section text with the approved review-of-final-judgments language.
provisions.evidence: Section IV(2) of the Act amends 28 U.S.C. 1257.
provisions.notes: Approved plan exists and is sequenced after PL-17-133.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 28 U.S.C. 1291.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section text with the approved jurisdiction-of-appeals language.
provisions.evidence: Section IV(4) of the Act amends 28 U.S.C. 1291.
provisions.notes: Section IV(3) has no independent Code operation.

provisions.text_summary: Direct amendments to Public Law 17-135 and Public Law 4-32, not to the Code.
provisions.evidence: Section V amends Public Law 17-135 and Public Law 4-32 directly.
provisions.notes: No Code-text operation available for this phase.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch already contains the amended title 28 text at sections 1253, 1257, and 1291, with the associated USAR amendment notes.

recommended_actions: Leave the title 28 amendments in place.
Do not create any separate Code operation for Section V.
Retain the current history note trail for the already-incorporated sections.

laws.title: Public Law 22-163 | Employee's Bill of Rights
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The Act creates federal-employee protections, a D.C. District Court appellate mechanism, and penalties for unlawful treatment. No express Code amendment is identified, so it belongs as a statutory note.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, effective date, conflicting-legislation clause, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: This Act may go into effect upon its signature of ratification by the President.

provisions.text_summary: False-termination and retaliation rules for federal employees.
provisions.evidence: No officer or employee of the United States shall take any personnel action.

provisions.text_summary: Worker's Appellate Body and D.C. District Court jurisdiction.
provisions.evidence: The United States District Court for the District of Columbia shall have original jurisdiction.

provisions.text_summary: Criminal penalties for prohibited personnel actions.
provisions.evidence: Any person who performs a personnel action prohibited under Section 2 ... shall be imprisoned.

current_implementation.detail: The current note treatment is directionally correct because the Act is a general federal-employment statute with no unique positive-law section target.

recommended_actions: Keep it as a statutory note at the closest employment-law anchor.
Do not force a fabricated Code amendment.
Remove any stale Trello reference if still attached.

laws.title: Public Law 22-164 | Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Claude's classification report preserved the act as a statutory note because no uniquely executable Code amendment exists. The report specifically places it at 50 U.S.C. 3352d. The current XML anchor is off-target if left at Title 18.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, definitions, and introductory security terms.
provisions.evidence: This act may be cited as the "Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act."

provisions.text_summary: ODNI authority, counterintelligence, clearance controls, reporting, and automated clearances.
provisions.evidence: The Office of the Director of National Intelligence shall be the sole authority.
provisions.notes: General national-security statute rather than a sectional Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall become effective immediately upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: No dedicated Pub. L. 22-164 note surfaced in the live tree search, even though the approved anchor is 50 U.S.C. 3352d.

recommended_actions: Relocate the statutory note to 50 U.S.C. 3352d.
Keep the act as note-only; do not invent positive-law section text.
Remove any stale Title 18 anchor or Trello reference.

laws.title: Public Law 23-165 | 2nd Amendment Protection Act (2APA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section 4(1) expressly amends 18 U.S.C. 930(g)(1), and Section 4(2) is a rule of construction with no independent host section. The act itself is not repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "2nd Amendment Protection Act."

provisions.text_summary: Repeals and replaces 18 U.S.C. 930(g)(1) with a D.C. roads-and-sidewalks exemption.
provisions.exact_change: Replace subsection (g)(1) text with the enacted Federal-facility definition including the D.C. road and sidewalk exemption.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(1) amends 18 U.S.C. 930(g)(1).
provisions.notes: Approved plan exists for this subsection.

provisions.text_summary: Rule of construction barring overbroad firearm bans in D.C.
provisions.evidence: 18 U.S.C. 930 ... must not be construed to completely ban firearm ownership.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch already contains the amended Federal facility definition in 18 U.S.C. 930(g)(1) and the associated USAR amendment note.

recommended_actions: Leave the amended 18 U.S.C. 930(g)(1) text in place.
Keep Section 4(2) as a note or commentary only; do not guess a host.
Preserve the existing history note trail.

laws.title: Public Law 23-166 | Amended Federal Employment Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable, and the classification record marks the law as repealed with no Code text changed. No operative statutory text could be reconstructed from the supplied source bundle.

provisions.text_summary: Source deleted or not found; no operative text recoverable.
provisions.evidence: Source deleted or not found.
provisions.notes: No lawful Code conclusion can be derived from the missing source.

current_implementation.detail: The existing note is historical only. Because the source is unavailable, no Code amendment should be inferred.

recommended_actions: Retain only as a historical note.
Do not invent or extrapolate any Code operation.
Keep the source-recovery flag open.

laws.title: Public Law 23-167 | Uh Oh Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The Act repeals Public Law 16-121 in its entirety and contains a broad series-level rule, but it does not self-repeal. It is therefore retained as current statutory history rather than as a positive-law Code rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, effective date, and background matter.
provisions.evidence: This act shall take effect immediately upon the signature of the President.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 16-121 and declares the 16th through 22nd series to remain in effect unless otherwise repealed.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 16-121 shall be repealed in its entirety.
provisions.notes: The series-wide clause is a rule of construction, not a Code amendment.

current_implementation.detail: No readable enactment text was recovered, so no Code operation is confirmed in the live tree.

recommended_actions: Keep as a statutory-history note only.
Apply repeal-marking to Public Law 16-121 where that act is carried.
Remove any stale note boilerplate if present.

laws.title: Public Law 23-168 | Affirming Innovation Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section 3(a) reoccupies 29 U.S.C. 208 with a new FLSA section 8 text, and Section 3(b) is intentionally uncodified because it does not identify a host section. The act itself is not repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and severability.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Affirming Innovation Act."

provisions.text_summary: Inserts a new FLSA section 8 at 29 U.S.C. 208.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the bare repealed placeholder with the new limitations on non-compete agreements text and update the title heading.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(a) amends the Fair Labor Standards Act by inserting a new section 8.
provisions.notes: Approved plan exists and is already reflected in the working tree.

provisions.text_summary: Enforcement and penalties for FLSA violations without a clear host section.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(b) does not identify a specific subsection or Code host.

current_implementation.detail: The working tree already contains the reoccupied section 208 note and the heading update. Section 3(b) remains uncodified, which matches the approved plan.

recommended_actions: Keep the section 208 insertion and title update as implemented.
Do not add a separate operation for Section 3(b).
Preserve the prior-provisions history.

laws.title: Public Law 23-169 | Military Readiness Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The Act is a general military-readiness statute with short title, severability, effective date, definitions, inspections, oversight, and enforcement, but no express Code amendment. It should be preserved as a statutory note.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be referred to as the "Military Readiness Act".

provisions.text_summary: Inspections, oversight, locations, and enforcement.
provisions.evidence: The DOD shall require units ... to perform monthly or bi-weekly inspections.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effectiveness language.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall take effect immediately upon Presidential approval.

current_implementation.detail: The note-only placement is appropriate, but the current note's source-limitation claim is wrong because the source text is recoverable and usable.

recommended_actions: Retain the statutory note at the Title 10 military anchor.
Correct the false source-limitation language if it remains in XML.
Do not create a positive-law section amendment.

laws.title: Public Law 23-170 | The Get Around DC Act
laws.status_basis: The source is a local/District enactment that does not supply a permanent Code amendment and is preserved only as statutory history. The classification record marks it as repealed or superseded.

provisions.text_summary: Local/District enactment with no positive-law Code target.
provisions.evidence: This enactment is preserved as a statutory note.

current_implementation.detail: The note-only treatment is correct for a local/DC enactment. No permanent positive-law text should be created.

recommended_actions: Keep only as a historical note.
Do not add positive-law Code text.
Remove the Trello link if still attached.

laws.title: Public Law 23-171 | Diplomatic Immunity Act
laws.status_basis: The source is unavailable in usable text form, and the later classification record identifies PL-031-227 as repealing this law before codification. No current Code insertion is made.

provisions.text_summary: Text not recovered from the scanned source.
provisions.evidence: The recovered source does not supply verifiable operative text, and PL-031-227 repeals Public Law 23-171.
provisions.notes: Retain only as a source-limited historical record.

current_implementation.detail: A note anchor exists in current XML, but the recovered source text is insufficient to verify any substantive codification result.

recommended_actions: Keep only a source-limited historical note.
Do not assert any substantive Code effect.
Remove the Trello link.
